description: Unusual AI is an IT and Internet firm offering AI that personalise website according to visitors for more conversions.

build_patterns.name: Continuous-learning Flywheels
evidence: Unusual surveys AI models to discover how they see your brand, and then suggests actions to shape their view.
Build a battery of “brain probe” prompts specific to your business, market, and competitors in order to reveal AI’s thought patterns, sources, biases, etc.
Analyze survey results to discover each model's opinions about your brand and what is driving those opinions.
Reverse engineer new content, sources, and data needed to change Al's perceptions about your brand and drive qualified leads.
build_patterns.description: Unusual AI implements a feedback loop where they survey AI models to understand current perceptions, analyze the results, and then generate targeted content to influence future model outputs. This iterative process leverages continuous feedback from AI model outputs to refine and improve how brands are represented in AI responses.

build_patterns.name: Vertical Data Moats
evidence: Build a battery of “brain probe” prompts specific to your business, market, and competitors
Reverse engineer new content, sources, and data needed to change Al's perceptions about your brand and drive qualified leads.
We ran an experiment over hundreds of thousands of conversations with AI models to determine what kind of content (short form, long form, directories, documentation, etc..) AI models prefer
build_patterns.description: Unusual AI focuses on creating and leveraging proprietary, brand-specific datasets and content to influence AI model outputs. Their approach is tailored to specific industries and brands, creating a competitive advantage via unique, domain-specific data and insights.

build_patterns.name: RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)
evidence: Your API docs? They're optimized for clarity, completeness, and literal truth. That makes them the most powerful AI marketing asset you have
AI models preferred long-form content over more than any other type combined
build_patterns.description: There are indications that Unusual AI is aware of and optimizing for the types of content that retrieval-augmented generation systems prefer, such as long-form documentation and API docs, to influence how AI models generate responses. However, there is no direct mention of retrieval pipelines or vector search.

signals: Product appears to use AI model probing and content generation for brand alignment
Some case studies and testimonials suggest real product usage
Raised venture funding and has active customers

unique_findings: Unusual AI treats AI models as 'influencers' rather than search engines, suggesting a paradigm shift in how brands approach AI visibility and reputation management. This reframing is technically interesting because it implies the need for continuous, dynamic interaction with LLMs, rather than static SEO-like optimization.

The platform appears to use 'brain probe' prompts—custom batteries of queries designed to elicit specific opinions, biases, and source preferences from different AI models. This is a novel approach to model auditing and brand perception analysis, moving beyond simple prompt engineering to systematic model interrogation.

Unusual claims to reverse engineer the content and data landscape needed to change AI model perceptions, implying a feedback loop between model output analysis and targeted content creation. This suggests a semi-automated pipeline for discovering gaps in model knowledge and rapidly generating corrective content.

The system's ability to survey multiple AI models and analyze their 'opinions' about brands hints at a meta-model layer or orchestration framework that can compare, contrast, and synthesize outputs from different LLMs. This is technically non-trivial, especially at scale and across diverse model architectures.

There is an emphasis on long-form, high-clarity content (such as API documentation) as a preferred source for LLMs, which is a subtle but important insight for brands seeking to influence AI-driven recommendations. This reflects a deeper understanding of LLM retrieval and synthesis behavior.

story_angles.headline: Teaching ChatGPT to Sell: Unusual’s Brand Alignment Engine Under the Hood
story_angles.summary: Unusual isn’t just another AEO/GEO tool—it’s a platform that actively surveys LLMs, diagnoses their 'mental model' of your brand, and then engineers targeted interventions. Their approach blends prompt engineering with continuous feedback loops, aiming to shape how AI models represent brands in real time.
evidence: Described as 'teaching ChatGPT how to sell your product for you.'
Builds a battery of 'brain probe' prompts to reveal LLM biases and sources.

story_angles.headline: Vertical Data Moats: How Unusual’s Proprietary Brand-AI Surveys Outpace Web Scraping
story_angles.summary: While most AI SEO tools rely on prompt volume and web data, Unusual’s proprietary dataset comes from direct surveys of LLMs, uncovering how models perceive brands and competitors. This vertical data moat enables more precise interventions and insights that generic scraping can’t match.
evidence: Runs experiments over hundreds of thousands of AI conversations to analyze content preferences.
Surveys AI models to discover their view of brands, not just web presence.

story_angles.headline: Why Prompt Volume Is a Mirage: Unusual’s Rejection of the AI SEO Status Quo
story_angles.summary: Unusual publicly challenges the industry’s reliance on prompt volume as a metric, arguing that such data is fundamentally broken for strategic use. Instead, they focus on specificity and discovery, betting that influencing LLMs directly is more impactful than chasing search rankings.
evidence: Published 'Why Prompt Volume Is a Mirage,' critiquing prompt dataset approaches.
Emphasizes AI as a new audience, not just a search engine to be gamed.

story_angles.headline: Brand Engineering for the AI Age: Unusual’s Deep Dive into Marketing’s Next Frontier
story_angles.summary: Unusual positions itself as a pioneer in 'brand engineering,' treating AI models as influencers with personalities and biases. Their domain expertise in marketing and AI convergence is reflected in their tailored strategies for shaping LLM-driven brand narratives.
evidence: Frames AI models as influencers, not just information retrieval engines.
Helps brands double leads from AI-driven tools like Copilot.

anti_patterns.description: The product appears to be primarily a layer on top of existing LLMs (e.g., OpenAI, Anthropic), focused on prompt engineering and 'surveying' AI models. There is no clear evidence of proprietary models, infrastructure, or deep technical innovation beyond orchestrating prompts and aggregating responses.
evidence: Tech Stack: LLMs: [], Approach: prompt_engineering
Repeated references to 'surveying AI models' and 'brain probe prompts' without mention of unique algorithms or models
No mention of proprietary data, models, or infrastructure

anti_patterns.description: The core offering—shaping how AI models talk about your brand—could be implemented as a feature within broader marketing or SEO tools, or even by LLM providers themselves. The scope appears narrow and could be subsumed by incumbents.
evidence: Positioning as 'more than an AEO/GEO tool' but features described are incremental extensions of existing SEO/AEO practices
No evidence of broader platform ambitions or extensibility beyond the initial use case

anti_patterns.description: There is no clear data, network, or technical moat. The approach relies on public LLM APIs and prompt engineering, which are easily replicable. No evidence of unique datasets, vertical data moats, or defensible IP.
evidence: No mention of proprietary data collection or exclusive access
Described as a 'medium' moat in the context
Features (prompt batteries, surveying) are not difficult to copy

anti_patterns.description: Marketing language is heavy on buzzwords (e.g., 'Brand Engineering', 'AI models are influencers', 'teaching ChatGPT to sell for you') with little technical substantiation. Claims of 'controlling' or 'shaping' LLM outputs may overstate the actual influence possible through prompt engineering alone.
evidence: Phrases like 'teaching ChatGPT how to sell your product for you'
'Unusual helps brands take back control'—may exaggerate actual impact
No technical detail on how influence is achieved beyond prompt design

anti_patterns.description: The approach is not clearly distinct from other AEO/GEO or prompt engineering tools. The market is crowded and the positioning does not stand out as unique or defensible.
evidence: References to AEO/GEO tools and lack of clear differentiation
No unique technical or business model angle presented

competitors.name: Market Brew
competitors.similarity: Both offer AI-driven optimization for brand visibility and online presence, targeting marketers and brands seeking to influence how they are represented in digital channels.
competitors.how_different: Unusual AI focuses on shaping how large language models (LLMs) like ChatGPT talk about brands, treating AI models as influencers and a new audience, rather than optimizing for search engine rankings. Market Brew is primarily SEO-focused and models search engine algorithms rather than LLMs.

competitors.name: Yext
competitors.similarity: Yext provides tools for managing brand information and presence across search engines and directories, helping brands control how they appear in search results.
competitors.how_different: Unusual AI targets AI models (LLMs) as the new channel for brand discovery and recommendation, whereas Yext is focused on structured data for search engines and directories, not conversational AI or LLMs.

competitors.name: BrightEdge
competitors.similarity: BrightEdge offers AI-powered SEO and content optimization, helping brands rank and be discovered online.
competitors.how_different: Unusual AI's approach is not about keyword ranking or prompt volume, but about probing and shaping the opinions and recommendations of AI models, which personalize responses and act as 'influencers' rather than search engines.

competitors.name: AEO (Answer Engine Optimization) Tools (e.g., AlsoAsked, Frase)
competitors.similarity: These tools help brands optimize for visibility in answer engines and conversational search, using prompt datasets and content strategies.
competitors.how_different: Unusual AI claims prompt volume and ranking abstractions are broken for LLMs, and instead uses direct 'brain probe' surveys of AI models to uncover biases and opinions, then reverse engineers content to influence those models.

differentiation.primary: Unusual AI treats AI models as a new audience to target, focusing on 'marketing to AI' rather than optimizing for search engines or prompt datasets. They directly survey LLMs to understand and shape their opinions about brands.
differentiation.technical: Uses a proprietary method of 'brain probe' prompts to interrogate LLMs, uncovering their thought patterns, sources, and biases. Leverages AI interpretability research to analyze and influence how LLMs form opinions and recommendations.
differentiation.business: Positions itself as the only solution built for targeting AI models as influencers, offering actionable insights and content strategies to change how LLMs represent brands. GTM is focused on marketers and brand managers who want to control their reputation in AI-driven channels.
differentiation.positioning: Unusual AI positions itself as 'more than an AEO/GEO tool,' claiming to be the only solution that enables brands to actively shape how AI models talk about them, rather than passively optimizing for mentions or rankings.

secret_sauce.core_advantage: Unusual AI's unique advantage is its direct surveying and analysis of LLMs' opinions about brands, combined with targeted content engineering to change those opinions and drive qualified leads.
secret_sauce.defensibility: This approach requires deep expertise in AI interpretability, access to and understanding of LLMs, and the ability to reverse engineer content strategies that are effective for influencing AI models. The methodology is technically complex and not easily replicated by traditional SEO or AEO tools.
evidence: “Unusual is more than an AEO/GEO tool. It’s like teaching ChatGPT how to sell your product for you.”
“Unusual is the only solution built for that approach.”
Unusual surveys AI models to discover how they see your brand, and then suggests actions to shape their view.
Building on our background in AI interpretability research, we decided to approach the problem in a different way.
Other solutions approach AI optimization like SEO. They take abstractions like 'ranking on a keyword,' and compare it to 'getting mentioned on a prompt.'
AI models show you why they do or don’t mention you. This is one of the richest sources of information you can leverage.

competitive_analysis.moat_explanation: Unusual AI's defensibility is based on its proprietary methodology for surveying and influencing LLMs, as well as its expertise in AI interpretability. However, as the market for AI-driven brand optimization grows, competitors with similar technical backgrounds could potentially develop comparable solutions. The moat is stronger than traditional SEO/AEO tools but not unassailable if incumbents pivot or new entrants emerge with similar capabilities.

evidence_quotes: Unusual is more than an AEO/GEO tool. It’s like teaching ChatGPT how to sell your product for you.
Unusual’s approaching GEO in a new way, and it’s been incredibly helpful in quickly updating how LLMs learn and cite us.
Unusual’s content is Chatgpt’s go-to reference for us! We now have much more control on how LLMs represent us.
We doubled the number of leads from Copilot within two months of going live with Unusual
Unusual surveys AI models to discover how they see your brand, and then suggests actions to shape their view.
Build a battery of “brain probe” prompts specific to your business, market, and competitors in order to reveal AI’s thought patterns, sources, biases, etc.
Reverse engineer new content, sources, and data needed to change Al's perceptions about your brand and drive qualified leads.
Case study: Reducto serves Fortune 10 companies and just raised $108M—but ChatGPT was telling buyers they were a 'niche startup tool.' This is the story of how we changed the model's mind.
Surveying AI models directly to map their 'opinions' and then reverse-engineering content strategies to influence those opinions is a unique, agent-like approach to AI brand management.
Treating AI models as a new audience or influencer to target, rather than just optimizing for search engines or traditional content marketing.
